name: Pork Chops Stuffed with Caraway Rye
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Rye bread with caraway seeds
4 Loin pork chops, each about 1-in. thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300°F. Put the bread cubes in a baking dish and place in the oven. Bake for about 5 minutes or until lightly browned.
2. Meanwhile, melt the butter in a saucepan and add 1 cup of chopped onion, garlic, and mushrooms. Cook, stirring, until wilted. Spoon the mixture into a mixing bowl and add parsley, caraway seeds, bread cubes, and egg. Stir well.
3. Holding a knife parallel to the surface of the meat, split each chop in half to the bone, top to bottom, but do not cut entirely through. Place the flaps of each chop on a flat surface and pound with a mallet until even in thickness. Stuff each chop with equal portions of stuffing. Fold the flaps together to enclose the stuffing and secure with wooden toothpicks.
4. Heat oil in a heavy skillet large enough to hold the chops in one layer without crowding. Add the chops and cook over moderately high heat about 4 minutes on each side until well browned. Continue cooking, turning occasionally, about 15 minutes. Transfer the chops to a hot serving dish and keep warm.
5. Pour off the fat from the skillet and add the remaining onion. Cook, stirring, for about 1 minute, then add vinegar and stir well. Add broth and bring to a boil, stirring.
6. Remove toothpicks from the chops. Strain the sauce through a sieve, pushing as much of the solids as possible through, and serve.
